evidence_grounding.reasoning: Iter 2 lands the belt-and-braces grounding stack promised in the v1 roadmap. gemini.py:150-219 adds _check_evidence_grounding (any 3-word window of finding.evidence must appear in webpage_context, normalized casefold+whitespace; explicit escape hatch for JS-rendered/dynamic/not-observable via _UNOBSERVABLE_PHRASES) and _check_competitor_leak (grammatical title-cased multi-word regex _TITLECASE_MULTIWORD scanned over findings+strengths strings, whitelisted against brief.competitors OR the crawled page, with _PACKET_SAFELIST for section headers / journey stages / scoring dimensions to kill scaffolding false positives). service.py:32,50-51 now threads webpage_context and brief.competitors through run_review_for_brief — previously dropped with `_webpage_context`. run_review (gemini.py:276-302) injects a targeted 'Semantic validation failed…' Retry Note with the top-3 issues on failure and accepts on the final attempt to avoid starvation. The checks auto-disable when both references are empty so offline/no-crawl cases don't false-positive. Residual gaps: (a) evidence check only runs on findings, not on strengths/score reasons/strength.evidence; (b) the final-attempt accept path means a persistent leak or ungrounded evidence can still ship — it's a tradeoff vs. starvation but worth a one-line 'soft warning' log; (c) the 3-word window is a bag-of-trigrams, not a quote-preserving check, so paraphrased evidence that happens to share any trigram can pass. Still best-practice-shaped; approaching audit-trail-grade.

persona_grounding_and_voice.reasoning: agent.py:216-221 inserts a '## Voice Check (do not skip)' block immediately before '## Execution Instructions' naming persona.name and the top-5 voice anchors, with the explicit directive that auditor-voice sentences must be rewritten. agent.py:261 adds a Reflection-Loop 'Voice audit' bullet requiring first-person rewriting of any sentence that could plausibly appear in a generic UX audit. This is exactly strategy rq-local-007 — voice reminder placed in the generation-zone token window, plus a reflection-time re-check. Three residual gaps keep this off 5: (1) the voice check only covers persona_voice + persona_reason, not the review_summary / first_impression / why_it_matters fields where auditor drift is also common; (2) there is no code-level voice check (e.g., pronoun audit, anchor-presence regex) — all enforcement remains prompt-only so regressions under fallback-builder conditions aren't caught; (3) the persona fallback at service.py:119-134 with generic anchors ('practical, time-conscious, skeptical, low-hype') remains the synthetic-persona-theater risk if Gemini enrichment fails. Core lift solid; polish gaps remain.

finding_severity_discipline.reasoning: Unchanged from iter 1 — no new changes in the diff for agent.py:241-246 Blocker/High/Medium/Nit anchors or the Reflection-Loop blocker-cap. The iter-1 behavioral rubric remains strong: explicit 'Do NOT use High as a default', Nielsen-triad reconstruction requirement (frequency + magnitude + persistence reconstructible from impact_on_user + impact_on_business), '~1 Blocker per 5 findings' cap, 'if every finding is High, downgrade the weakest', and the 'if the journey works cleanly it is correct to contain only Medium/Nit' anti-inflation clause. Headroom remains code-level enforcement of the blocker-cap and a triad-completeness check in the validator — neither attempted in iter 2. Score held at 4.0.

scoring_rubric_calibration.reasoning: Unaddressed in iter 2. agent.py still lists the 8 SCORING_DIMENSIONS as bare names (task_clarity, task_success, effort_load, trust_confidence, value_communication, error_recovery, accessibility, emotional_fit) with no per-dimension definition and no 1/3/5 behavioral anchor table per strategy rq-local-002. The schema still enforces integer 1-5 but no score↔reason consistency check exists; temperature=0.1 from iter 1 is the only indirect defense against central-tendency collapse. This is now the single highest-leverage remaining lift — purely prose additions in build_review_packet with zero structural risk, and criterion is active in the mid phase.

business_goal_anchoring.reasoning: Unaddressed in iter 2. Schema still requires expected_business_outcome on each prioritized_improvements entry, and Execution Instructions #1/#7-10 plus the Reflection Loop bullet 'EVERY recommendation is tied to the stated business goal' exert strong prompt pressure. But no token-overlap check (rq-local-003 step) has been added to validate that expected_business_outcome textually references brief.business_goal. The severity rubric's 'High = directly threatens the stated business_goal' phrasing creates incidental reinforcement. Mid-phase priority; cheap lift available.

validation_semantic_coverage.reasoning: Mixed. The structural validate_review_output (gemini.py:351-359) is unchanged — still only checks top-level non-emptiness, so a single-field finding with priority='High' and nothing else still passes validation. HOWEVER, the new semantic post-checks (_check_evidence_grounding + _check_competitor_leak) functionally act as a partial per-item semantic floor for the two highest-value failure modes (ungrounded evidence, leaked product names), and they DO feed targeted Retry Notes back into the retry loop — closing a major gap in iter-1 semantic-retry diagnostics. Still missing: (a) per-finding FINDING_REQUIRED non-empty field check (every finding has problem+persona_voice+impact_on_user+impact_on_business+improvement_direction populated); (b) journey_stage enum enforcement (JOURNEY_STAGES still not an enum in review-output-schema.json despite strategy rq-local-005 being trivial); (c) priority enum spot-check in the validator as a backstop to the schema; (d) business-goal token-overlap on quick_wins. This criterion is deferred in mid phase but its score is rising due to the semantic post-checks that landed for other criteria.

feedback: Iteration 2 executed exactly the roadmap from iter-1 memory: (1) voice carry-through near Execution Instructions + Reflection-Loop voice-audit bullet (agent.py), and (2) threaded webpage_context + allowed_competitors through run_review with _check_evidence_grounding and _check_competitor_leak post-filters in gemini.py, plus service.py wiring. Both are well-engineered: the grounding check has a proper unobservability escape hatch, the competitor leak detector is grammatical (not hardcoded brand list) with a thoughtful _PACKET_SAFELIST to prevent scaffolding bleed-through, and the retry hint now carries top-3 specific issues instead of 'structurally empty'. The final-attempt accept path is a defensible starvation-vs-strictness tradeoff. Aggregate should move from 3.06 → ~3.73 (well on track for 4.0 by iter 3-4). Top remaining lifts in priority order: (a) per-dimension 1/3/5 anchors for the 8 scoring dimensions (strategy rq-local-002, pure prose in agent.py — highest ROI remaining), (b) journey_stage enum in review-output-schema.json (trivial, rq-local-005), (c) business-goal token-overlap check in validate_review_output (rq-local-003 partial, cheap), (d) persona_voice / finding-field non-empty checks inside validate_review_output to formalize what the post-filter already implies.

priority_fixes.suggestion: Add a '## Scoring Rubric' block to build_review_packet (after '## Scoring Dimensions', before '## Voice Check'). For each of the 8 dimensions (task_clarity, task_success, effort_load, trust_confidence, value_communication, error_recovery, accessibility, emotional_fit), provide a one-line definition of what the dimension measures from the persona's perspective and concrete anchors for score 1 (failing), 3 (adequate), and 5 (excellent) — tied to the persona's core_journey and business_goal. This resolves central-tendency collapse by converting 'what feels right' into 'which row matches the evidence I cited in the reason field'. Also enforce reason-before-score ordering in the schema template so chain-of-thought precedes the numeric commit (the schema currently emits reason and score together; make the LLM write reason first).

Extend _check_evidence_grounding to also cover strengths[].evidence (currently only findings are scanned) and scores[dim].reason if the reason quotes specific copy. Optionally widen the window check from a strict trigram to a 3-gram OR a quoted-span match (detect content between straight/curly quotes and require that the quoted span itself exists in webpage_context) — this catches the common hallucination pattern 'the hero says "Sign up free"' where the LLM fabricates a direct quote. Add a one-line log warning (stderr or raw_output sidecar) when the final-attempt accept path ships with unresolved semantic_issues so reviewers can audit the soft-accept cases.

Add a token-overlap check in run_review: for each entry in prioritized_improvements.quick_wins / structural_fixes / validation_experiments, verify that expected_business_outcome shares at least one content token with brief.business_goal (exclude stopwords: the/a/an/to/and/or/of/in/for/with/on). Thread brief.business_goal into run_review alongside the existing webpage_context/allowed_competitors kwargs. Surface token-overlap failures through the same semantic_issues retry channel so the model receives specific feedback ('quick_win #2 expected_business_outcome does not mention the stated business goal').

Two lightweight follow-ups: (1) extend the '## Voice Check' block to also cover review_summary.first_impression and review_summary.why_it_matters — these are first-person-heavy fields currently outside the voice reinforcement; (2) add a tiny code-level voice probe that checks each finding.persona_voice for at least one of persona.voice[:5] (case-insensitive substring), surfacing failures as semantic_issues. Optional: replace the generic service.py:119-134 fallback persona voice anchors with a 'FALLBACK PERSONA — voice unavailable' marker so downstream reviewers aren't misled by synthetic-but-plausible anchors.

Add a lightweight blocker-cap enforcement in the semantic post-check: if len([f for f in findings if f.priority=='Blocker']) > max(1, len(findings)//5), raise a semantic_issue asking the model to downgrade the weakest Blockers. Also spot-check that each finding's impact_on_user AND impact_on_business are both populated strings (not empty, not one is a copy of the other) — this formalizes the iter-1 Nielsen-triad rubric at code level without requiring triad parsing.
